body: Three Indonesian companies, PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi TBK, PT Bangun Kosambi Sukses TBK (CBDK), and PT Delta Giri Wacana TBK (DGWG), will list on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on Monday, having raised IDR 59.5 billion, IDR 2.3 trillion, and IDR 202.94 billion, respectively, from their initial public offerings. The listings bring the total number of listed companies on the exchange to 951, with the IDX aiming to reach 1,000 by the end of the year.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange to Welcome Three New Listings from Diverse Sectors on Monday

body: Foreign investors traded 3 billion shares on Friday, January 10, with a net sell of 754.26 million shares in PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) and 25.26 million shares in PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA), while net buying 109.28 million shares in PT Bumi Resources Mineral Tbk (BUMI) and 50 million shares in PT Maharaksa Biru Energi Tbk (MRE). Other notable transactions included net sells in PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) and PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), and net buys in PT Alamtri Resources Indonesia Tbk (ALMI), PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL), and PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA).
title: Foreign investors trade 3 billion shares in Indonesian market, with notable buys and sells in various sectors.

body: PT Aneka Tambang Tbk (ANTM) reported a preliminary unaudited exploration expenditure of IDR 271.49 billion for the period ending December 31, 2024, with exploration activities focused on commodities such as gold, nickel, and bauxite. The company's exploration efforts aim to ensure the adequacy of its strategic mineral resources and reserves, supporting the continuity of its operations and the development of its nickel downstream business.
title: PT Aneka Tambang announces 2024 exploration report, highlighting activities and expenditures for gold, nickel, and bauxite exploration in various regions of Indonesia.

body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) secured the top spot in net foreign buy, with 109.28 million shares, resulting in a stock price closing at IDR 428, its peak for the day, with a total trading value of IDR 358.70 billion. PT Maharaksa Biru Energi Tbk (OASA) ranked second, with a net foreign buy of 49.66 million shares, and a stock price reaching IDR 150 at its highest point, with a total trading value of IDR 21.67 billion.
title: PT Bumi Resources Minerals leads in net foreign buying with 109.28 million shares.

body: Inflation pressure in Indonesia is expected to increase in February 2025, with the price expectation index rising to 160.2 points, but is forecast to ease in May 2025 to 151.1 points. Meanwhile, retail sales are predicted to decline in both February and May 2025, with the sales expectation index dropping to 127.7 points and 138.6 points, respectively.
title: Bank Indonesia's survey predicts higher inflation in February 2025, but easing in May 2025, alongside expected decline in retail sales.

body: PT Bayan Resources Tbk (BYAN) spent USD 2.49 million on mining exploration activities in three areas through its subsidiaries, PT Wahana Baratama Mining, PT Perkasa Inakakerta, and PT Tanur Jaya, in the fourth quarter of 2024. The exploration activities, conducted by third-party companies including PT Elnusa Tbk (ELSA) and PT Kwarsa Sentosa Abadi, resulted in a total of 47 drilling points and 4,800 meters of drilling at the Sepaso Beruang blocks, and 48 shallow drilling points and 9 deep drilling points at the Tanur Jaya concession area.
title: Bayan Group Spends USD 2.49 Million on Mining Exploration in Three Areas in Q4 2024.

body: PT Platinum Wahab Nusantara Tbk (TGUk), owner of the Teguk beverages brand, has closed over 100 outlets in 2024, retaining only 35 stores, due to unfavorable market conditions and decreased purchasing power among middle-low-income households. The company reported owning 152 outlets at the end of 2023, but the number dropped to 83 in June 2024 and further to 35 in October 2024, forcing the company to implement efficiency measures.
title: PT Platinum Wahab Nusantara Closes Over 100 Teguk Outlets Due to Challenging Market Conditions.

body: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi TBK (CUAN) saw its share price surge 12.19% in a single day, reaching IDR 13,800 per share, driven by positive market sentiment following favorable news about the company, including the start of metallurgical coal production by its subsidiary and a credit facility from PT Bank Negara Indonesia TBK (BBNI). The company's financial performance has been strong, with a 612.75% year-on-year revenue surge to USD 546.06 million and a 162.70% increase in net profit to USD 30.44 million in September 2024.
title: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi's share price surges 12.19% in a single day due to positive market sentiment and favourable news about the company.

body: PT Cahayasakti Investindo Sukses Tbk (CSI) is set to carry out a rights issue of up to 3.9 billion shares with a nominal value of Rp100 per share, a plan approved by shareholders on January 9, 2025, with an 80.12% quorum. As of December 2024, the company had 1.30 billion shares, with PT Olympic Kapital Equity holding 22.77%, PT Andalan Utama Bintara holding 57.23%, and public holding 20%.
title: PT Cahayasakti Investindo Sukses Tbk to Conduct Rights Issue of 3.9 Billion Shares.

body: The Indonesian government has partnered with Qatar-based Qilaa Internasional Group to build 1 million affordable housing units for low-income households in urban areas, with funding to be provided through a government-to-government scheme between Indonesia and Qatar. The project is part of a larger 3 million housing program initiated by President Prabowo Subianto, with the government set to provide land and apartment buildings, prioritizing regions such as Jakarta, Banten, and West Java.
title: Indonesian government partners with Qatar-based investor to build 1 million affordable housing units for low-income households.
